eng: Actions speak louder than words
english: What you do is more important than what you say
meaning: People are judged by their actions rather than their promises or statements.

eng: A stitch in time saves nine
english: Fix problems early to avoid bigger issues
meaning: Taking care of a small problem immediately prevents it from becoming a major one later.

eng: Better late than never
english: It’s better to do something late than not at all
meaning: Even if delayed, completing a task is more valuable than leaving it undone.

eng: Don’t count your chickens before they hatch
meaning: Avoid making plans based on outcomes that haven’t yet happened.

eng: Every cloud has a silver lining
english: There is something good in every bad situation
meaning: Even difficult situations can have positive aspects or outcomes.

eng: Honesty is the best policy
english: Being truthful is always the best approach
meaning: Truthfulness builds trust and avoids complications.

eng: Practice makes perfect
meaning: Consistent effort leads to mastery over time.

eng: Time is money
meaning: Wasting time can lead to lost opportunities and productivity.

eng: When in Rome, do as the Romans do
meaning: It is wise to follow the practices of the place you are in.

eng: Where there’s a will, there’s a way
meaning: Strong desire and effort can overcome obstacles.

eng: You reap what you sow
meaning: Good or bad outcomes depend on your actions.

eng: A penny saved is a penny earned
english: Saving money is as valuable as earning it
meaning: Careful spending helps build wealth over time.

eng: Too many cooks spoil the broth
english: Too many people can ruin a task
meaning: Excessive involvement can lead to confusion and poor results.

eng: Birds of a feather flock together
meaning: People with common interests or traits tend to associate.

eng: The early bird catches the worm
meaning: Those who start early gain advantages over others.

eng: Don’t put all your eggs in one basket
english: Avoid risking everything in one place
meaning: Diversifying reduces the risk of total loss.

eng: Beggars can’t be choosers
english: Those in need must accept what they get
meaning: When options are limited, one cannot be picky.

eng: The pen is mightier than the sword
english: Words are more powerful than force
meaning: Communication and ideas can create greater impact than violence.

eng: Look before you leap
meaning: Careful consideration prevents mistakes.

eng: Slow and steady wins the race
meaning: Persistent effort over time achieves goals better than rushing.

eng: All that glitters is not gold
english: Not everything that looks valuable is valuable
meaning: Appearances can be misleading.

eng: An apple a day keeps the doctor away
meaning: Regular care of health helps avoid diseases.

eng: Don’t bite the hand that feeds you
english: Don’t harm those who help you
meaning: One should be grateful and respectful to supporters.

eng: A picture is worth a thousand words
meaning: Visuals can communicate ideas more effectively than long explanations.

eng: Out of sight, out of mind
meaning: People tend to forget what they don’t see regularly.

eng: No pain, no gain
meaning: Hard work and sacrifice are necessary to achieve goals.

eng: Easy come, easy go
english: Easily gained things are easily lost
meaning: Things obtained without effort are often not valued.

eng: Fortune favors the brave
meaning: Taking risks can lead to success.

eng: The grass is always greener on the other side
meaning: People often think others have it better than they do.

eng: Necessity is the mother of invention
meaning: Challenges and needs drive creative solutions.
